description: Wave 2 — production seams and open-source readiness. v1 shipped a deterministic core engine, event-sourced replay, async runner, OpenSkill ratings, and a FastAPI admin surface against SQLite + mocked LLMs. Wave 2 closes the gaps that block self-hosting and a shared central backend: runner writes ground-truth seats / game status, provider secrets resolve from env or files, LiteLLM is exercised against recorded cassettes from real providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cerebras, DeepInfra, Ollama), per-role canonical prompts replace the demo placeholder, an async scheduler drains pending gauntlets with backpressure and retries, API gets pagination + scoped API-key auth + rate limiting, Postgres becomes a first-class target with a passing Alembic suite, a `padrino bootstrap` CLI brings a fresh deployment to ready in one command, Prometheus metrics + scheduler health endpoints make ops observable, signed game-export bundles plus a hash-chain-verifying ingestion endpoint enable a federated public leaderboard, a docker-compose stack runs the whole thing locally, and release CI publishes versioned images. Quality gates from v1 remain unchanged (ruff, ruff format, mypy strict, pytest, 85% core-engine coverage, AST purity + no-wallclock firewalls); a new `live_llm` pytest marker gates the recorded-provider contract job. Hard rules from v1 are inherited verbatim: nothing under src/padrino/core/ may import db/llm/api/runner or call wall-clock / network / random; mafia chat is parsed only via the `action` field; ranked observations never leak role, faction, model identity, ratings, clone index, or other games' transcripts.

userStories.title: Runner persists game_seats and Game.status atomically
userStories.description: As an operator, I need every game run — demo, API, scheduler — to write canonical seat assignments and a terminal status into the DB so leaderboards, transcripts, and downstream tooling never have to fall back to scanning game_events for GameTerminated.
acceptanceCriteria: Move the seat-backfill logic out of `padrino.demo_gauntlet._backfill_seats` into `padrino.runner.game_runner.GamePersistence`; on every `RolesAssigned` event, write one `game_seats` row per seat with role/faction sourced from the event payload

On `GameTerminated`, in the same `session.begin()` that already writes the event row + rating updates (US-039 pattern), set `Game.status='completed'` and `Game.terminal_result` (JSON: winner, reason, day_terminated) so a single txn covers event + ratings + game-row finalization

Replace every `event_type='GameTerminated'` lookup under `padrino.api` and `padrino.gauntlets` with a `Game.status='completed'` filter; `padrino.gauntlets.diagnostics_for_games` should accept already-terminal game IDs from the new path
Remove `_backfill_seats` from `demo_gauntlet.py` once the runner does it; `tests/cli/test_demo_gauntlet.py` and `tests/api/test_leaderboard_routes.py` must still pass without that helper
`tests/runner/test_game_persistence_seats.py` runs a full game and asserts game_seats matches `RolesAssigned.payload.assignments` exactly (role + faction + public_player_id)

`tests/runner/test_game_persistence_terminal.py` asserts Game.status + terminal_result are committed atomically with the GameTerminated row (kill the rating-update call mid-txn and assert nothing is persisted)
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
uv run coverage report --include='src/padrino/core/*' --fail-under=85 passes
userStories.notes: Game.status uses uppercase 'COMPLETED' to match the existing Gauntlet.status convention (CREATED/RUNNING/COMPLETED); the story's lowercase example was treated as illustrative. Game.terminal_reason column was dropped — reason now lives inside the JSON terminal_result.

userStories.title: Provider secret resolution with env and file schemes
userStories.description: As a self-hosting developer, I need `ModelProvider.auth_secret_ref` to actually resolve to a credential at adapter-construction time so I can rotate keys via env vars or mounted secret files without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: New module `padrino.llm.secrets` with `resolve_secret(ref: str) -> str` supporting `env:VAR_NAME` (read os.environ, error if unset) and `file:/abs/path` (read file, strip trailing whitespace, error if missing or world-readable on POSIX)
`padrino.llm.litellm_adapter.LiteLlmAdapter.__init__` calls `resolve_secret(provider.auth_secret_ref)` once and stores the resolved value; never re-reads per request

Invalid `auth_secret_ref` (unknown scheme, missing env var, unreadable file) raises `SecretResolutionError` at construction, never at request time — server boot fails loudly rather than silently 401-ing later
`tests/llm/test_secrets.py` covers: env hit, env miss, file hit, file miss, file with bad permissions, unknown scheme, empty value, and tilde / relative-path rejection
`tests/api/test_admin_routes.py` is updated so any provider creation in tests uses a valid `env:` ref or is monkeypatched to skip resolution
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: LiteLlmAdapter now requires a keyword-only `auth_secret_ref: str`; it calls `padrino.llm.secrets.resolve_secret` at construction and stashes the resolved value on `self._auth_secret` (never re-read per request). POST /model-providers also resolves the ref at creation so deployments with a misconfigured env: var or file: path 422 immediately rather than 401-ing later. `tests/api/test_admin_routes.py` uses an autouse monkeypatch fixture to set CEREBRAS_API_KEY and X for the duration of each test.

userStories.title: LiteLLM contract tests against recorded provider cassettes
userStories.description: As a developer adding a new model, I need a recorded-cassette test that proves the real LiteLLM adapter parses each provider's response shape so I learn about schema drift in CI rather than during a live gauntlet.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `pytest-recording` (or `vcrpy` directly) as a dev dependency; cassettes live under `tests/llm/cassettes/<provider>/<test_name>.yaml`
New marker `live_llm` registered in `pyproject.toml`; default `pytest` invocation deselects it, only the recorded-cassette path runs

`tests/llm/test_litellm_contract.py` parametrizes over OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini), Anthropic (claude-haiku-4.5), Cerebras (zai-glm-4.7), DeepInfra (DeepSeek-V4-Flash), and Ollama (llama3) — each test sends one canonical mini7 observation and asserts the returned object validates against `LlmResponse` and round-trips through `coerce_response_failure` cleanly when intentionally malformed
Re-recording flow documented inline: setting `PADRINO_RECORD_LLM=1` plus the provider env var re-records cassettes with secrets scrubbed; CI never records

Cassettes scrub `authorization`, `x-api-key`, and any `api_key` JSON fields before write — enforced by a `before_record_response` hook and an audit assertion in the test that scans for known secret-shaped substrings
CI workflow gains a separate job `live-llm-cassettes` that runs `uv run pytest -m live_llm` with no network access (cassettes only); the main job continues to skip the marker
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Used vcrpy (8.1.1) over pytest-recording for fewer moving parts. Cassettes live at tests/llm/cassettes/<provider>/{canonical,malformed}_response.yaml. The `live_llm` marker is registered in pyproject; tests/conftest.py installs a pytest_collection_modifyitems hook that default-skips live_llm items unless --live-llm or `-m live_llm` is passed. vcrpy 8.x ships httpcore stubs (not the older httpx_stubs name) which transparently intercept litellm's async httpx transport for all five providers. Cassettes were hand-authored against the actual provider URLs observed by probing litellm (openai → api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions; anthropic → api.anthropic.com/v1/messages; cerebras → api.cerebras.ai/v1/chat/completions; deepinfra → api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/chat/completions; ollama → localhost:11434/api/generate — note /generate not /chat for the `ollama/` prefix). CI gains a separate `live-llm-cassettes` job that runs `uv run pytest -m live_llm --live-llm`.

userStories.title: Per-role canonical prompts and resolution at game start
userStories.description: As a benchmark consumer, I need each seat to receive a prompt tailored to its role family so leaderboard role-family breakdowns reflect actual prompt-quality differences and not the shared `demo-prompt-v1` placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: Author canonical mini7 prompts under `src/padrino/llm/prompts/mini7_v1/<role_family>.md` for each RoleFamily (TOWN_INVESTIGATOR, TOWN_PROTECTOR, TOWN_CITIZEN, MAFIA) — prompts cover phase rules, action schema, win conditions, and explicit "never reveal your role unless strategically chosen" wording
New helper `padrino.llm.prompts.load_canonical(ruleset_id, role_family) -> PromptTemplate` reads the bundled markdown via `importlib.resources`; never touches disk at request time after first load
`AgentBuild` resolution at game start picks the prompt by `(ruleset_id, role_family)` after `RolesAssigned`; if a build pins a specific `prompt_id` that one wins, otherwise canonical is used
Seed migration `src/padrino/db/migrations/versions/<rev>_seed_canonical_prompts.py` inserts one Prompt row per role family with `version='canonical_mini7_v1'`

`tests/llm/test_prompt_resolution.py` asserts (a) every role family resolves to a distinct prompt, (b) explicit AgentBuild.prompt_id overrides canonical, (c) ranked-observation safety: prompts never include role-leak forbidden keys when concatenated with the observation projection
`tests/llm/test_canonical_prompts.py` lints every bundled prompt: non-empty, < 4 KB, no forbidden tokens ("detective", "doctor", "mafia") in TOWN_CITIZEN prompt, etc.
Update `padrino.demo_gauntlet` to use canonical prompts; leaderboard JSON now has non-empty `role_family_breakdown`
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
uv run coverage report --include='src/padrino/core/*' --fail-under=85 passes
userStories.notes: Followed the actual `RoleFamily` enum (DECEPTIVE / INVESTIGATIVE / PROTECTIVE / VANILLA_TOWN); the story's TOWN_INVESTIGATOR/etc. labels were illustrative. Per-seat prompt resolution lives in `LiteLlmAdapter.system_prompts_by_role: Mapping[Role, str] | None`; when set, the per-call system prompt is `mapping[obs.you.role]` with a fallback to the static `system_prompt` arg. An AgentBuild that pins a non-canonical prompt routes through the static `system_prompt` arg (no mapping). The bundled-prompts directory `padrino/llm/prompts/mini7_v1/` had to be renamed-aliased inside `padrino.llm.prompts.__init__` (`from padrino.core.rulesets import mini7_v1 as _mini7_ruleset`) because subpackage import otherwise rebinds the parent namespace's `mini7_v1` attribute to the empty subpackage. The `prompt_versions` table is unchanged (no new column); the 4 canonical rows all carry `version='canonical_mini7_v1'` and are distinguishable by the `developer_prompt` column which now stores the RoleFamily enum value. Leaderboard `role_family_breakdown` shape: `{role_family_value: {games, wins, draws, losses, win_rate}}`.

userStories.title: LLM retry / backoff with bounded attempts and dead-letter
userStories.description: As an operator running real-provider games, I need transient LLM failures (rate limit, 5xx, connection reset) to retry with bounded exponential backoff and a dead-letter outcome on exhaustion so a flaky provider doesn't deterministically lose a clone.
acceptanceCriteria: New module `padrino.llm.retry` with `RetryPolicy(max_attempts: int, base_delay_s: float, max_delay_s: float, retry_on: tuple[type[BaseException], ...])` and an async `with_retry(call, policy, sleeper)` helper; `sleeper` is injected (defaults to `asyncio.sleep`) so tests can pin time

Default policy retries on `litellm.RateLimitError`, `litellm.APIConnectionError`, `litellm.InternalServerError`, and `asyncio.TimeoutError`; never retries on `litellm.BadRequestError` or `AuthenticationError`
Backoff is deterministic given the SeededRng on the per-call context — jitter draws from `SeededRng(call_id)` not `random` — so replay still reproduces the exact retry schedule

On exhaustion, the adapter returns a structured `LlmCallFailed` outcome that the runner converts to `ActionTimedOut` with `reason='llm_exhausted'`; the original error chain is captured in `llm_calls.error_kind` + `error_message`
Per-call structured log line `llm.call.retry` with attempt_number, delay_ms, error_kind binds the existing contextvars (US-048)

`tests/llm/test_retry.py` covers: success on first attempt, success on retry, exhaustion, non-retryable error short-circuits, deterministic jitter given seed, structlog event emission, and that `tick.py` records exactly one `ActionTimedOut` per exhausted call (not one per attempt)
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: `padrino.llm.retry` exposes `RetryPolicy`, `RetryExhausted`, `RetryAttempt`, `LlmCallFailed`, `default_retry_policy()`, `with_retry()`, and `DEFAULT_RETRY_ON`. `with_retry(call, policy, *, sleeper, rng)` injects both time and randomness — `sleeper` defaults to `asyncio.sleep` on the adapter; jitter draws from `padrino.core.engine.rng.SeededRng` (no `random` import) seeded with `f'{observation.phase}:{observation.you.player_id}:{model_id}'`. Default policy: 3 attempts, 0.5 s base, 16 s cap, retry on RateLimitError / APIConnectionError / InternalServerError / TimeoutError. Import path is `from litellm.exceptions import ...` (NOT `import litellm; litellm.RateLimitError`) — mypy strict rejects the latter as `attr-defined` even with the `litellm.*` ignore_missing_imports override. AdapterResult gained `status='exhausted'` and `failure: LlmCallFailed | None`; tick.py inspects status=='exhausted' and emits ONE `ActionTimedOut` event with `payload.reason='llm_exhausted'`, `error_kind`, `error_message`, `attempts`. Two new nullable string columns `llm_calls.error_kind` and `llm_calls.error_message` added via migration 0006; `record_call(...)` accepts both. Existing `test_both_failures_yield_coerced_safe_response_in_vote_phase` updated to use two RuntimeErrors (the original mix included TimeoutError which is now retryable and would consume 3 fallback attempts instead of 1).

userStories.title: Async scheduler loop for pending gauntlets
userStories.description: As an API caller, I need POST /gauntlets to return immediately while a background worker actually runs the games so the request path stays sub-second and a long-running gauntlet survives an API restart.
acceptanceCriteria: New module `padrino.runner.scheduler` exposing `async def run_scheduler(session_factory, concurrency: int, stop_event: asyncio.Event) -> None`; loop pulls `Gauntlet.status='pending'` rows ordered by created_at, transitions to `running`, schedules child games under an `asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)`, and on completion sets `Gauntlet.status='completed'` plus a `completed_at` timestamp

Crash recovery: on startup, any `Gauntlet.status='running'` row whose `heartbeat_at` is older than 2 × the heartbeat interval is reset to `pending` and re-enqueued; heartbeat is written every 5 s while a gauntlet is in flight
New CLI: `padrino scheduler` (Typer command) runs the loop with `--concurrency`, `--db-url`, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM via the stop_event

POST /gauntlets in `padrino.api.gauntlet_routes` no longer runs games inline — it inserts the pending row and returns 202 with `Location: /gauntlets/{id}`; existing inline-execution test is replaced with an integration test that spins up the scheduler in-process

Observability events `scheduler.tick`, `scheduler.gauntlet.started`, `scheduler.gauntlet.completed`, `scheduler.heartbeat` are emitted via the US-048 seam; canonical names registered in `padrino.observability.events`

`tests/runner/test_scheduler.py` covers: pending → running → completed happy path, concurrency cap honored (asserted via an injected semaphore observer), crash recovery resets stale running rows, SIGTERM completes in-flight games before shutdown (mocked via stop_event)
`tests/api/test_gauntlet_routes.py` updated: POST returns 202, GET reflects scheduler-driven state transitions
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: API pagination, filtering, and cursor-stable ordering
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I need every list endpoint to paginate with stable cursors and accept basic filters so a deployment with thousands of games stays usable.
acceptanceCriteria: `padrino.api.pagination.CursorPage[T]` Pydantic model with `items: list[T]`, `next_cursor: str | None`, `total_estimate: int | None`; cursor is an opaque base64-encoded `(created_at, id)` tuple

Every list endpoint under `padrino.api.*_routes` (games, gauntlets, leaderboards, agent-builds, providers, prompts, model-configs) accepts `?limit=<1..200, default 50>&cursor=<opaque>` and returns `CursorPage`
Filter params per route: games `?status=&gauntlet_id=&ruleset_id=`, gauntlets `?status=&league_id=`, leaderboards `?gauntlet_id=&provisional=`; unknown params 422 (Pydantic strict)
Cursor is opaque and deployment-stable across schema migrations that don't drop `created_at` or `id`; tampered cursors return 400 with `invalid_cursor`

`tests/api/test_pagination.py` covers: page-through 250 rows with limit=50 yields all rows in stable order, filter narrows results, invalid cursor 400s, limit above bound 422s, and that two concurrent inserts during pagination don't produce duplicates or skips
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Scoped API-key authentication with per-key rate limiting
userStories.description: As a self-hosting operator, I need a real auth model with scoped API keys and per-key rate limits so an admin key, a submitter key, and a public spectator key can coexist without leaking write access.
acceptanceCriteria: New table `api_keys (id, key_hash, scopes JSON, label, created_at, last_used_at, disabled_at)`; raw keys are never stored, only `sha256(key)` hashed and a 6-char prefix for display

Scopes are `admin` (full read+write), `submitter` (POST /ingest only), `spectator` (read-only); `auth_dep(required_scopes)` FastAPI dep validates the `Authorization: Bearer pk_...` header and enforces scope intersection
Existing US-044 `X-Admin-Token` path is preserved as a back-compat shim behind a deprecation header `Deprecation: true; sunset=2027-01-01`; new deployments use API keys exclusively

Per-key sliding-window rate limit (in-process token bucket, configurable via `Settings.padrino_rate_limit_per_minute`; default 600 for admin, 60 for submitter, 1200 for spectator); 429 with `Retry-After` header on exhaustion
Admin endpoints to create / list / disable keys: `POST /admin/keys`, `GET /admin/keys`, `DELETE /admin/keys/{id}`; the POST response is the only place the raw key is ever returned

`tests/api/test_auth.py` covers: scope enforcement (admin key can write, submitter can't write to /admin/*, spectator can't write at all), key disabled → 401, rate limit returns 429 then resets after the window, raw key never appears in any GET response, hashed comparison is constant-time
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Postgres as a first-class target with passing Alembic suite
userStories.description: As an operator running shared infrastructure, I need Padrino to run identically on Postgres so I can scale beyond SQLite's single-writer limit and use managed-database backups.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `asyncpg` to runtime dependencies; `padrino.db.base.create_engine` auto-selects the driver from the URL scheme (`postgresql+asyncpg://` and `sqlite+aiosqlite://` both supported)

Replace every `sqlite_json.JSON` with `sqlalchemy.JSON` (driver-portable); on Postgres this maps to `JSONB`, on SQLite to `JSON` text — confirmed via `Column.type.python_type` assertions in `tests/db/test_dialect_portability.py`
FK enforcement: the existing SQLite `PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON` connect listener stays; Postgres has FKs on by default — no driver-specific code paths beyond engine construction

Alembic migrations run cleanly on a fresh Postgres database: `tests/db/test_migrations_postgres.py` spins up `postgres:17-alpine` via `testcontainers`, runs `alembic upgrade head` then `alembic downgrade base` then `alembic upgrade head` again, asserts table schemas match expected
Same migration test runs on SQLite (existing path) so we don't regress; both are parametrized in `tests/db/test_migrations_all_dialects.py`

`tests/db/test_dialect_portability.py` runs the full repo CRUD against both SQLite and Postgres (via testcontainers, marked `@pytest.mark.postgres` so devs without docker can skip it locally; CI always runs it)
Settings gains `padrino_db_pool_size: int = 5` and `padrino_db_pool_max_overflow: int = 10`; both wired to `create_async_engine` only when the URL is Postgres
Documentation comment in `padrino.db.base.create_engine` explains the dialect branch; no separate Postgres-only module
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
uv run pytest -m postgres passes (CI only; documented as skipped locally without docker)

userStories.title: `padrino bootstrap` CLI for one-command deployment readiness
userStories.description: As a developer setting up Padrino for the first time, I need a single CLI command that takes me from a fresh database to a ready-to-serve deployment so the README quickstart fits on one screen.
acceptanceCriteria: New Typer command `padrino bootstrap` runs (in order, idempotent at each step): (1) `alembic upgrade head`, (2) seed canonical prompts if missing, (3) seed default mini7_v1 ruleset row, (4) optionally create an admin API key (`--with-admin-key` prints the raw key to stdout once), (5) optionally register providers from a YAML file (`--providers path.yaml`)
Providers YAML schema documented in-code and validated with Pydantic: `providers: [{name, base_url, auth_secret_ref, default_model, timeout_s}]`; unknown keys 422
Each step prints a single structured log line; the command is safe to re-run — already-seeded rows are skipped, not duplicated
Exit code 0 on full success, 1 on any step failure with the failing step name surfaced; partial-success scenarios document which steps completed

`tests/cli/test_bootstrap.py` covers: fresh DB end-to-end, re-run is a no-op, provider YAML happy path, provider YAML with invalid secret ref fails fast (US-050 integration), `--with-admin-key` prints exactly one key and stores its hash
README's quickstart is updated to reference `padrino bootstrap`
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Prometheus /metrics endpoint with canonical counters and histograms
userStories.description: As an operator wiring dashboards and alerts, I need Padrino to expose metrics in Prometheus text format so I can scrape latency, throughput, and error rates without parsing JSON logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `prometheus-client` as a runtime dep; new module `padrino.observability.metrics` defining Counter/Histogram/Gauge instances: `padrino_llm_calls_total{provider,model,status}`, `padrino_llm_latency_seconds{provider,model}` (histogram), `padrino_phase_duration_seconds{ruleset,phase_kind}` (histogram), `padrino_games_total{outcome,ruleset}`, `padrino_invalid_action_total{reason}`, `padrino_scheduler_inflight_gauntlets` (gauge), `padrino_api_requests_total{route,method,status}` (Counter via FastAPI middleware)
Metrics are updated at the same observability seams that emit US-048 structured logs — single call site, both outputs
`GET /metrics` returns text/plain; no auth required by default (Prometheus scrape pattern); a `Settings.padrino_metrics_require_auth: bool = False` flag gates it behind the spectator scope when set

`tests/observability/test_metrics_endpoint.py` covers: counters increment on simulated calls, histograms record observations, endpoint output parses with `prometheus_client.parser.text_string_to_metric_families`, auth flag works both ways
Existing `padrino metrics` CLI subcommand (US-048) continues to work — it reads from the DB as before; the endpoint is the live in-process view

uv run ruff format --check . passes

userStories.title: Scheduler health endpoint and worker lag visibility
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a health endpoint that surfaces scheduler liveness and queue lag so I can alert on a stuck or wedged worker.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /healthz/scheduler` returns 200 with `{status: 'ok'|'degraded'|'down', last_heartbeat_at, pending_gauntlets, running_gauntlets, oldest_pending_age_s}`; `degraded` if `oldest_pending_age_s > 60`, `down` if `last_heartbeat_at` is null or older than 30 s
`GET /healthz` (existing, US-041) is left alone — still a cheap liveness check; the new endpoint is the scheduler-specific readiness probe

Scheduler writes heartbeat to a new `scheduler_heartbeats (worker_id, beat_at)` table on each tick (US-054 heartbeat is per-gauntlet; this is per-worker); a worker_id is generated at startup from `socket.gethostname() + os.getpid()`
`padrino_scheduler_inflight_gauntlets` gauge (from US-059) is updated from the same heartbeat path
`tests/api/test_healthz_scheduler.py` covers: ok / degraded / down states by injecting heartbeats and pending rows, multiple workers reflected in the response
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Signed game-export bundle with hash-chain attestation
userStories.description: As a developer who ran a benchmark locally, I need to export a single signed artifact containing the full event log and metadata so I can publish results to a central backend or share them with a colleague and prove they weren't tampered with.
acceptanceCriteria: New module `padrino.export.bundle` with `export_game(session, game_id) -> GameBundle` returning a Pydantic model containing: ruleset_id, gauntlet_id, game_id, league_id, seed, agent_builds (resolved to display_name + prompt_version, no secrets), events (full event log in canonical order), game_seats, terminal_result, and `tip_hash` (the final event_hash in the chain)
Canonical serialization via `padrino.core.engine.canonical_json` (already byte-stable) so two exports of the same game are byte-identical

Optional Ed25519 signing: `export_game(..., signer=Ed25519Signer.from_env('PADRINO_EXPORT_PRIVATE_KEY'))` produces a `bundle.sig` over the canonical bytes; verification key fingerprint is embedded in the bundle as `signer_fingerprint`
CLI: `padrino export game <game_id> [--out path.json] [--sign]`; bundle is JSON with embedded base64 signature, never a binary format

`tests/export/test_bundle.py` covers: byte-identical re-export, hash-chain integrity (tampering any event invalidates `tip_hash` recomputation), signature verifies, signature over tampered bundle fails, secrets (auth_secret_ref) never appear, ranked-observation safety guard runs over every event payload

uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Public ingestion endpoint with hash-chain verification
userStories.description: As an operator of a central Padrino backend, I need a public submission endpoint that accepts signed game bundles, verifies the hash chain, and stores results so the community leaderboard reflects contributions without granting submitters write access to existing data.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /ingest/game` accepts a `GameBundle` (US-061 shape) and requires the `submitter` scope (US-056); admin scope also accepted

Verification pipeline: (1) signature check if `bundle.sig` present and the submitter has a registered Ed25519 public key, (2) hash-chain replay via `padrino.core.engine.replay.replay_event_log` recomputes every event_hash from the prior, (3) the recomputed tip must equal `bundle.tip_hash`, (4) the bundle's `game_id` must be globally unique (idempotent on retry returns 200 with `already_ingested: true`)

Stored rows: a new `ingested_games` table (separate from `games` so local runs and shared runs don't mix); rows reference the submitter API key id, the original gauntlet/league are stored as opaque strings (not FKs into local tables)

Submitter-key registration: `POST /admin/keys` (US-056) gains an optional `submission_public_key` field; a key without a registered pubkey can still submit but the bundle must be unsigned and is tagged `unverified` in storage
Rate limit (US-056) defaults to 30 submissions per minute per key; large bundles bounded at 10 MB JSON, rejected with 413 otherwise

`tests/api/test_ingestion.py` covers: signed happy path, unsigned + unverified storage, tampered event → 422 with `hash_chain_mismatch`, duplicate game_id idempotent, wrong scope → 403, oversized bundle → 413
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Public read-only API for ingested results and federated leaderboard
userStories.description: As a community member or dashboard consumer, I need read-only endpoints that aggregate ingested game bundles into a public leaderboard so anyone can compare model performance without an admin key.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /public/leaderboard` requires only `spectator` scope (or no auth if `Settings.padrino_public_leaderboard_anonymous=True`); returns paginated entries (US-055 cursor) ordered by conservative_score desc, computed across ingested_games filtered by `ruleset_id` and optional `gauntlet_id`

`GET /public/games/{id}` returns the full ingested bundle minus any submitter PII; `GET /public/games/{id}/events` paginates the event log; `GET /public/games/{id}/transcript` returns the ranked-observation-safe transcript projection (US-039 guard reused)
`GET /public/submitters` lists registered submitter labels + pubkey fingerprints + game count; never reveals raw keys or contact info

Aggregation is recomputed on read via a cached helper `padrino.ratings.public_leaderboard(session, ruleset_id, gauntlet_id, cursor, limit)`; cache key includes `max(ingested_games.created_at)` so new submissions invalidate naturally

`tests/api/test_public_routes.py` covers: leaderboard sorting + cursor stability, transcript projection drops forbidden keys (`role`, `faction`, model identity, ratings), anonymous-access flag toggles auth requirement, submitter listing excludes raw keys
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Docker image and docker-compose dev stack
userStories.description: As a self-hosting developer, I need a single `docker compose up` to launch the API, scheduler, and Postgres so trying Padrino doesn't require setting up a Python environment.
acceptanceCriteria: `Dockerfile` (multi-stage: `uv` install layer → slim runtime) builds an image that contains `padrino`, `padrino scheduler`, `padrino bootstrap`; entrypoint is `padrino` so the same image serves API and scheduler via different commands

`docker-compose.yml` at repo root defines services `api`, `scheduler`, `postgres` (postgres:17-alpine with a volume), wires `PADRINO_DB_URL` to the postgres service, runs `padrino bootstrap` as a one-shot init container before `api` and `scheduler` come up
`.env.example` documents every required env var with safe defaults; `docker-compose.override.yml.example` shows how to bind-mount a `providers.yaml` and a host secret file (US-050 `file:` scheme)
Image is < 250 MB compressed, runs as a non-root user `padrino:padrino`, healthchecks via `GET /healthz` and `GET /healthz/scheduler`

`tests/docker/test_compose_smoke.py` (marked `@pytest.mark.docker`, skipped without docker) brings up the stack, hits `/healthz` and `/metrics`, runs a demo gauntlet via the API, and tears down; CI runs this on push to main
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Central-backend deployment recipe and shared-leaderboard docs
userStories.description: As an operator wanting to host the canonical Padrino leaderboard, I need a documented recipe for deploying the public surface so submitters know where to point and the threat model is explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: New `docs/deployment/central-backend.md` (the one new doc file the wave explicitly authorizes) covering: minimum viable deployment (managed Postgres + container host), DNS + TLS, admin key bootstrap, public-leaderboard flag, submitter onboarding (key generation + pubkey registration), backup and restore via `pg_dump`

New `docs/deployment/self-host.md` covering: local docker-compose, BYO-model via providers.yaml, secret file mounts, troubleshooting the most common boot failures (bad secret ref, migration drift, port conflict)
New `docs/deployment/byo-model.md` covering: adding a new provider, recording cassettes (US-051), prompt customization, and the role of `AgentBuild` vs `ModelConfig`

Each doc ends with a verified runbook (commands actually run in `tests/docs/test_runbooks.py` against a docker-compose harness where feasible; bash blocks tagged `# verified` are extracted and asserted to exit 0)
README's top-level overview gains a short "deployment options" table linking to the three guides; everything in README beyond quickstart moves to these docs
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Release CI: cross-platform smoke, image publish, and version bumping
userStories.description: As a maintainer cutting an open-source release, I need CI to run the full gauntlet on supported platforms and publish a versioned container image so each tag is a known-good artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub Actions workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml` triggers on `v*` tags: runs the existing test matrix on `ubuntu-latest` + `macos-latest` for Python 3.12 and 3.13, builds the Dockerfile, publishes to GHCR as `ghcr.io/<owner>/padrino:<version>` and `:latest`

Workflow `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (existing or new) runs on every push/PR: ruff check + format, mypy, pytest, core-coverage gate, AST firewalls, `live-llm-cassettes` job (US-051), `postgres` job via testcontainers (US-057), `compose-smoke` job (US-064)

Semantic version source-of-truth in `pyproject.toml`; `padrino version` (existing) reads it via `importlib.metadata`; a release-checklist script `scripts/release.sh` runs `uv lock --frozen`, `uv run pytest`, builds, and prompts for tag push
Image is signed with cosign keyless OIDC; the public verification key fingerprint is documented in `docs/deployment/central-backend.md` so operators can pin
`CHANGELOG.md` follows Keep-a-Changelog; the release workflow refuses to publish if `## [Unreleased]` is empty for the version being cut

`tests/release/test_metadata.py` asserts `padrino version` matches `pyproject.toml`, the entry-points are stable, and that no test file imports a private (underscore-prefixed) module from another package boundary
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
